notes: Based on the official Epiphan userguide tech-specs page (primary source) plus the manufacturer product page. The Pearl-2 is available as three SKUs: ESP1150 (portable desktop), ESP1151 (1 RU rackmount), and ESP1152 (1 RU rackmount twin/dual-channel). rackUnits of 1 applies to ESP1151/ESP1152 only; the desktop ESP1150 is not rack-mountable. The 4 XLR audio inputs are documented as female XLR accepting balanced and unbalanced signals; the spec does not explicitly state XLR3 vs combo XLR/TRS, but standard pro-audio XLR3 is the near-certain form. RCA audio: documented as 2× RCA female (1 stereo pair, -10 dBV). RS-232 is listed as female; DB9 is the universally standard physical connector for RS-232 on AV equipment and confirmed by Epiphan RS-232 control documentation, but the sub-type is inferred. Power inlet connector type is not explicitly stated in specs; internal 100–240 VAC PSU ships with a region-specific detachable power cord, consistent with an IEC C14 inlet (standard for this class of gear), which is inferred. HDMI Output 1 is HDMI 2.0 and Output 2 is HDMI 1.4a per the official spec page. The 2× USB-A rear ports are for UVC/UAC (webcams/USB audio) and the 1× USB-A front port is for general use/USB storage. Network RJ-45 is 10/100/1000 Base-T (Gigabit).

ports.name: USB In 1 (UVC)
ports.note: Rear panel; UVC/UAC USB 3.0 for webcams and USB audio devices

ports.name: USB (Front)
ports.note: Front panel; USB 3.0 for storage or peripherals

ports.name: Ethernet
ports.note: 10/100/1000 Base-T; used for streaming, recording, NDI, SRT, RTSP, and admin

ports.name: RCA In L
ports.note: Consumer line level -10 dBV; left channel of stereo pair

ports.name: RCA In R
ports.note: Consumer line level -10 dBV; right channel of stereo pair

ports.name: RS-232
ports.note: Female; DB9 connector inferred (standard RS-232 physical connector); 19200 bps; for third-party control

ports.name: AC In
ports.note: IEC C14 inlet inferred; internal universal PSU 100–240 VAC 50–60 Hz; region-specific detachable power cord included
